{{Quote|The Imperial bombardment continues, but it looks like they're moving forward with an attack from the planet surface. This raid is much more intense than usual. We're evacuating the last of the command staff…|General Jan Dodonna|<ref name="Rebel Strike III" />}}
Following the destruction of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] at the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] '''invaded''' the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Great Temple/Legends|base]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] in an attempt to capture the [[Alliance High Command/Legends|Rebel leaders]] as an act of retribution.

==Prelude==
In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] had struck a large blow against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] when Rebel [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] attacked and destroyed the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] during the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]].<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> Some time after, the Empire reacted to the Death Star's demise by sending a large Imperial force to invade the Rebel base on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]].<ref name="Rebel Strike III Guide" />

==The invasion==
[[File:TBoY_ImpInvasion.jpg|thumb|left|180px|The Galactic Empire invades [[Yavin Prime/Legends|Yavin's]] fourth moon with landing craft and TIE fighters.]]
The Imperials sent a larger attack than usual against the base, determined to crush the Rebels at last. [[Red Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Red Squadron]], led by [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], were ordered to take out the [[Imperial Action transport|Imperial transports]] that were landing on the planet.<ref name="Rebel Strike III Guide" />

{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike}}
During Red Squadron's attack on the Imperial transports, Skywalker, before destroying the fourth transport, briefly entered the immense ship with his X-wing to pick up advanced [[Deflector shield/Legends|shield technology]] for future use, having spotted them.<ref name="Rebel Strike III Guide" />
{{Endgame}}

Upon the destruction of the remaining four transports, the invasion was temporarily stalled. A [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighter]] then ambushed Luke only for it to be shot down by [[Red Three/Legends|Red Three]], [[Sarkli]]. Afterward, the second wave of Imperial transports, consisting of [[Imperial loader shuttle|loader shuttles]], arrived to strengthen the ground forces. The Rebel [[General/Legends|General]] [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna]] then ordered Red Squadron to shoot down the loader shuttles, as well as defend the final Rebel transport [[Luminous/Legends|''Luminous'']] that was currently docked on one of the islands near the temple until it was ready for takeoff. Afterward, the ''Luminous'' managed to escape. After a majority of the loader shuttles and [[Dropship/Legends|dropships]] were destroyed, a transport slipped through defenses and deployed a [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] force into the [[Great Temple/Legends|Great Temple]], pinning General Dodonna in the Ceremonial Chamber. Skywalker ordered Sarkli to defend the transports, while he and [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] landed in the temple and [[Lightfight|shot]] their way through the attackers.<ref name="Rebel Strike III" />

[[File:TBoY_RedSquadron.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Red Squadron ready to attack the Imperial invaders.]]
The two managed to save [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Bren Derlin/Legends|Bren Derlin]] and Dodonna, and a squad of [[Rebel trooper/Legends|Rebel troopers]], and proceeded to the entrance of the base, where they were pinned down by two [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-STs]]. Sarkli swooped down and destroyed the walkers, allowing the group to escape in a [[Unidentified GR-75 medium transport (Invasion of Yavin 4)|GR-75 medium transport]]. General Dodonna then thanked Skywalker, as he felt it was because of Skywalker sending Sarkli off that there were survivors.<ref name="Rebel Strike III" />

==Aftermath==
However, there was still an Imperial [[Yavin blockade|blockade]] awaiting them in space. In addition, [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] expressed his displeasure at the turn of events, although Darth Vader revealed that it was only a temporary setback. Red Squadron was next [[Mission to Dantooine|deployed]] to Dantooine to aid the defection of [[Tycho Celchu/Legends|Tycho Celchu]].<ref name="Rebel Strike III" /> Although an Imperial victory, the battle was also seen as a Rebel victory, because the Rebel command was safely evacuated. However, not all Rebel commanders were evacuated during this battle, two examples being [[Bothan High Command]] and General [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna]], who were still on Yavin IV after the battle.<ref name="X-W: TKT">''[[X-Wing: The Krytos Trap]]''</ref><ref name="Battlefront II">''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]''</ref> Bothan High Command was destroyed during another [[Strike on Yavin 4|battle]]<ref name="Battlefront II" /> whilst Jan Dodonna was captured during the [[Evacuation of Yavin|final battle of Yavin]].<ref name="SW:GB">''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|The Emperor has ordered a raid against the Rebel base on the Yavin moon. He wishes for you to lead the assault and remind them of the Empire's might, Lord Vader.|Briefing narrator narrating the mission description for "Revenge on Yavin 4"|<ref name="Rebel Strike III" />}}
{{Quote|The Imperials have landed on Yavin 4, and are heading toward the Rebel base. Stall the Empire's advance and buy the Rebels time to escape.|Briefing narrator narrating the mission description for "Revenge of the Empire"|<ref name="Rebel Strike III" />}}
In the [[2001]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron II: Rogue Leader]]'', a level called "Revenge on Yavin" takes place on Yavin's fourth moon. The level sees the player, as Darth Vader, attempt to destroy several Rebel [[GR-75 medium transport/Legends|GR-75 medium transports]] before they can leave the moon's surface. They are defended by [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|BTL Y-wing starfighters]] and [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]], and occasionally, [[T-47 airspeeder/Legends|T-47 airspeeders]] can be seen speeding across the moon's surface. Additionally, in this level, the planet Yavin is seen in background a purple color, rather than the usual orange. This level, as well as a similar level which had the player take control of Vader during the Battle of Yavin to prevent the Death Star's destruction, were apparently intended to tease the planned spinoff game ''[[Star Wars: Dark Squadron]]'' before the latter game was ultimately passed over in favor for [[Untitled Chewbacca game|another game]] starring Chewbacca.<ref name="Unseen64">{{WebCite|url=https://nerdist.com/2-previously-unknown-star-wars-video-games-surface|text=2 Previously Unknown STAR WARS Video Games Surface|author=Marnell, Blair|date=2016-08-16|work=[[Wikipedia:Nerdist Industries|Nerdist]]|archivedate=20171229142101}}</ref> The canonical battle, called "Revenge of the Empire", appears in the [[2003]] video game ''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike]]'', with the player flying as Luke Skywalker. In addition, the bonus mission was likewise omitted from the co-op mode for ''Rebel Strike'' (which was itself a port of ''Rogue Leader''), presumably to avoid confusion.

The level was designed by Jamie Urmacher, who wanted to create a basic level for players who hadn't played a Rogue Squadron game before Rebel Strike. As such, the TIEs were also added in so the players could rack up points for medals and have a challenge. As there was no reference from the movie, Steve Huelsback composed original music for the level. In ''[[The Making of Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike]]'', when covering various gaffes and hiccups experienced during development as a result of "reinventing the wheel" regarding the engine for ''Rogue Leader'', one of the gaffes shown was Luke in the Yavin Temple running around erratically while his head was slightly floating unattached to his main body.
